ucs.title: [LLM] Wizard Spider / FIN12 AdFind reconnaissance batch (adf.bat) preceding Ryuk/Conti deployment
ucs.description: Hunts the signature AdFind.exe enumeration sequence (trustdmp, objectcategory=*, subnets, dclist) that Wizard Spider operators run from a wrapper batch file (adf.bat / adfind.bat) on a Cobalt Strike beachhead host minutes-to-hours before lateral movement and Ryuk/Conti deployment. CrowdStrike, Mandiant (UNC1878/FIN12), and DFIR Report engagements consistently fingerprint this exact sequence as a Wizard Spider trademark.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdlines dc(Processes.process) as distinct_cmds from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=adfind.exe OR Processes.original_file_name=AdFind.exe OR Processes.process="*adfind*") AND Processes.process IN ("*trustdmp*","*objectcategory=person*","*objectcategory=computer*","*objectcategory=organizationalUnit*","*objectcategory=group*","*dcmodes*","*adinfo*","*dclist*","*subnets*","*-gcb*","*-sc*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name host
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| where distinct_cmds >= 3 OR match(parent_cmdlines, "(?i)(adf|adfind|recon|enum)\.bat")
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
| eval actor="Wizard Spider / FIN12 / UNC1878"
ucs.kql:
let adfindCmds = dynamic(["trustdmp","objectcategory=person","objectcategory=computer","objectcategory=organizationalUnit","objectcategory=group","dcmodes","adinfo","dclist","subnets","-gcb","-sc"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName =~ "adfind.exe" or ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName =~ "AdFind.exe" or ProcessCommandLine has "adfind"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (adfindCmds)
| extend BatchParent = iff(InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe") and InitiatingProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(adf|adfind|recon|enum)\.bat", true, false)
| summarize FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp), Cmds=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,15), DistinctCmds=dcount(ProcessCommandLine), ParentCmds=make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine,5) by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName
| where DistinctCmds >= 3 or ParentCmds has_any ("adf.bat","adfind.bat","recon.bat")
| extend Actor="Wizard Spider / FIN12 (Periwinkle Tempest)"
ucs.rationale: AdFind itself is dual-use, but the specific multi-flag enumeration burst (trustdmp + objectcategory=* + dcmodes + subnets) launched from a 'adf.bat' wrapper is a documented Wizard Spider/UNC1878 playbook artefact reused across Ryuk, Conti, and BazarLoader intrusions. Legitimate admin use of AdFind almost never executes 3+ of these queries within seconds from a batch wrapper, making this a high-fid

ucs.title: [LLM] Wizard Spider Ryuk/Conti pre-encryption sabotage chain (vssadmin + wbadmin + bcdedit + service stop)
ucs.description: Detects the rapid, multi-tool recovery-sabotage sequence Wizard Spider runs seconds before Ryuk or Conti encryption: vssadmin/wmic shadowcopy delete, wbadmin delete catalog, bcdedit recoveryenabled no / bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures, and net stop of backup/AV services. The clustering of these specific commands on a single host within a short window is the canonical Wizard Spider (GOLD BLACKBURN / ITG23 / FIN12) ransomware staging fingerprint.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmds values(Processes.process_name) as tools values(Processes.user) as users from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ( (Processes.process_name=vssadmin.exe AND Processes.process IN ("*delete shadows*","*resize shadowstorage*")) OR (Processes.process_name=wmic.exe AND Processes.process="*shadowcopy delete*") OR (Processes.process_name=wbadmin.exe AND Processes.process IN ("*delete catalog*","*delete systemstatebackup*","*delete backup*")) OR (Processes.process_name=bcdedit.exe AND Processes.process IN ("*recoveryenabled no*","*bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures*")) OR (Processes.process_name=net.exe AND Processes.process="*stop*" AND Processes.process IN ("*sql*","*veeam*","*backup*","*sophos*","*sentinel*","*defender*","*acronis*","*mssql*")) ) by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name _time span=10m
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| stats min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime dc(tools) as distinct_tools values(tools) as tools values(cmds) as cmds values(parent_process_name) as parents by dest
| where distinct_tools >= 2
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
| eval actor="Wizard Spider (Ryuk/Conti pre-encryption)", severity="critical"
ucs.kql:
let window = 10m;
let sabotage = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe"  and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("delete shadows","resize shadowstorage"))
     or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe"      and ProcessCommandLine has "shadowcopy delete")
     or (FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe"   and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("delete catalog","delete systemstatebackup","delete backup"))
     or (FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe"   and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("recoveryenabled no","bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures"))
     or (FileName =~ "net.exe"       and ProcessCommandLine has "stop" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("sql","veeam","backup","sophos","sentinel","defender","acronis","mssql","memtas","mepocs"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
sabotage
| summarize FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp), DistinctTools=dcount(FileName), Tools=make_set(FileName), Cmds=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,20), Parents=make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName) by DeviceId, DeviceName, bin(Timestamp, window)
| where DistinctTools >= 2
| extend Actor="Wizard Spider / FIN12 / GOLD BLACKBURN (Ryuk/Conti staging)", Severity="Critical"
ucs.rationale: Individually each command can be benign or appear in other ransomware playbooks, but the co-occurrence of >=2 of these specific binaries (vssadmin/wmic/wbadmin/bcdedit/net stop of backup-AV services) on one host inside a 10-minute window is the hardcoded Ryuk and Conti pre-encryption routine that has been embedded in Wizard Spider's tooling since 2018 and reproduced almost identically across CISA
